Lines.FullText: Creates a search filter that accepts all objects. "Visit children" always returns `true`.

Elements.RawText: Creates a search filter that accepts all objects. "Visit children" always returns `true`.

Brief.RawText: * @brief Creates a search filter that accepts all objects. "Visit children" always returns `true`.

Lines.FullText: Creates a "conjunction" search filter that combines 2 filters, accepting an object only if both filters accept it.

Elements.RawText: Creates a "conjunction" search filter that combines 2 filters, accepting an object only if both filters accept it.

Lines.FullText: "Visit children" returns `true` only if both filters do so.

Elements.RawText: "Visit children" returns `true` only if both filters do so.

Brief.RawText:
* @brief Creates a "conjunction" search filter that combines 2 filters, accepting an object only if both filters accept it.
 * "Visit children" returns `true` only if both filters do so.

Elements.RawText: The first argument of the conjunction operation.

Elements.RawText: The second argument of the conjunction operation.

Tags.RawText: @param right The second argument of the conjunction operation.

Lines.FullText: Creates a "disjunction" search filter that combines 2 filters, accepting an object if any of the two filters accepts it.

Elements.RawText: Creates a "disjunction" search filter that combines 2 filters, accepting an object if any of the two filters accepts it.

Lines.FullText: "Visit children" returns `true` if any of the two filters accepts does so.

Elements.RawText: "Visit children" returns `true` if any of the two filters accepts does so.

Brief.RawText:
* @brief Creates a "disjunction" search filter that combines 2 filters, accepting an object if any of the two filters accepts it.
 * "Visit children" returns `true` if any of the two filters accepts does so.

 * This filter constructor should always be the final filter wrapper, and should not be used as a constructor argument
 * for another filter.
